title: Nested if
subtitle: An if statement inside another if
goal: Learn to use one if statement inside another for multi-level checks.
explanation: Nested if means an if statement placed inside another if.
The outer condition is checked first.
Only when it is true does the inner if run.
This lets you build more complex conditions step by step.
Nested if statements are often used to check access, roles, and user permissions.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: To enter a VIP area, you must first pass through the main entrance. Only then is your VIP status checked.

remember: The inner if is checked only after the outer one passes.
Nested if means an if statement inside another if.
It lets you create checks that run in sequence.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners may think all if statements are checked at the same time. In fact, the inner if runs only after the outer one passes.

quiz.question: When does the inner if run?
options: When the outer if returns true
Only when the outer if returns false
